title: Nizamia observatory
tagline: Nizamia observatory — heritage city of Telangana

overview.short: Nizamia observatory is an optical observatory built by Nawab Zafar Jung Bahadur of the House of Paigah and Amir of the Khurshid Jahi estate and established by Mahbub Ali Khan, Asaf
overview.description: Nizamia observatory is an optical observatory built by Nawab Zafar Jung Bahadur of the House of Paigah and Amir of the Khurshid Jahi estate and established by Mahbub Ali Khan, Asaf Jah VI - the 6th Nizam of Kingdom of Hyderabad in 1901. It participated in the Carte du Ciel making 463,542 observations. It boasted of an 8" Cooke Astrograph and a 15" Grubb refractor telescope.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Telangana State Guesthouse
topPlaces.description: Telangana State Guesthouse, formerly residential part of Chief Minister's Camp Office, is a state guest house of the State Government of Telangana, India. The camp office's office is converted to the Office of the Inspector General (security).

topPlaces.name: Somajiguda
topPlaces.description: Somajiguda is a locality of Hyderabad located on either sides of Raj Bhavan Road. It started as a residential locality but slowly transformed into a modern business centre in Hyderabad.

topPlaces.name: Greenlands Guest House
topPlaces.description: Greenlands Guesthouse is a government-owned guesthouse located in Greenlands, Begumpet, in Hyderabad. It is a notified heritage structure in Hyderabad.

topPlaces.name: Hyderabad Central
topPlaces.description: Hyderabad Central is a shopping mall in Hyderabad, India, located on the Punjagutta road. The mall includes Marks and Spencer, as well as a range of movies, music, books, coffee shops, a food court, supermarket and restaurants.

topPlaces.name: Ameerpet metro station
topPlaces.description: Ameerpet metro station is an interchange metro station between the Red Line and Blue Line of the Hyderabad Metro. The Ameerpet Inter-change Metro Station is one of the largest metro stations in India with a sprawling premises over 200,000 square feet (19,000 m2).

topPlaces.name: Ameerpet
topPlaces.description: Ameerpet is a residential hub located in the north-west part of Hyderabad, Telangana. Ameerpet is also a Mandal in Hyderabad District.

topPlaces.name: Begumpet metro station
topPlaces.description: Begumpet ( also known as SBI Begumpet Metro Station) Metro Station is located on the Blue Line of the Hyderabad Metro in India. It is near to Begumpet railway station, Kundan Bagh, Country Club, Life style, Meena Bazaar Ext, Green park Hotel and Green lands road.

topPlaces.name: Parade Ground metro station
topPlaces.description: The Parade Ground Metro Station is located on the Blue Line of the Hyderabad Metro in India. It is part of Corridor I of the Hyderabad Metro starting from Miyapur and was opened to the public on 28 November 2017.

items.note: Telangana State Guesthouse, formerly residential part of Chief Minister's Camp Office, is a state guest house of the State Government of Telangana, India. The camp office's office is converted to the Office of the Inspector General (security).
Somajiguda is a locality of Hyderabad located on either sides of Raj Bhavan Road. It started as a residential locality but slowly transformed into a modern business centre in Hyderabad.
Greenlands Guesthouse is a government-owned guesthouse located in Greenlands, Begumpet, in Hyderabad. It is a notified heritage structure in Hyderabad.

Hyderabad Central is a shopping mall in Hyderabad, India, located on the Punjagutta road. The mall includes Marks and Spencer, as well as a range of movies, music, books, coffee shops, a food court, supermarket and restaurants.

Ameerpet metro station is an interchange metro station between the Red Line and Blue Line of the Hyderabad Metro. The Ameerpet Inter-change Metro Station is one of the largest metro stations in India with a sprawling premises over 200,000 square feet (19,000 m2).
Ameerpet is a residential hub located in the north-west part of Hyderabad, Telangana. Ameerpet is also a Mandal in Hyderabad District.

Begumpet ( also known as SBI Begumpet Metro Station) Metro Station is located on the Blue Line of the Hyderabad Metro in India. It is near to Begumpet railway station, Kundan Bagh, Country Club, Life style, Meena Bazaar Ext, Green park Hotel and Green lands road.

The Parade Ground Metro Station is located on the Blue Line of the Hyderabad Metro in India. It is part of Corridor I of the Hyderabad Metro starting from Miyapur and was opened to the public on 28 November 2017.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nizamia observatory?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nizamia observatory is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nizamia observatory?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Secunderabad Junction (Hyderabad) (5 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nizamia observatory?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nizamia observatory at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nizamia observatory?
faq.a: Stays in Nizamia observatory start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nizamia observatory?
faq.a: The top attractions are Telangana State Guesthouse, Somajiguda, Greenlands Guest House, Hyderabad Central, Ameerpet metro station.
